message:
fix(fr-store): zrangebyscore guards inverted range to avoid BTreeMap panic [bd-frankenredis-ot1z]

CRITICAL: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' against fr-server
panicked the runtime with:

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

The BYSCORE branch in fr-command::zrangestore_cmd parsed 0 and
-1 as score bounds (lower=0, upper=-1) and forwarded them to
store::zrangebyscore_withscores, which fed them to
BTreeMap::range without first checking start <= end. The
panic tore down the connection (and would tear down the worker
thread on a real workload — DOS vector from a single client).

Adds a score_bound_value helper and an early
"if min > max → empty" guard to both store::zrangebyscore and
store::zrangebyscore_withscores. Matches upstream behavior
(t_zset.c::genericZrangebyscoreCommand returns an empty range
when min > max).

Verified against vendored redis 7.2.4: 'ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1
BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) on both fr and upstream, and fr
remains alive afterwards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
